all_text: Final   qualifying   work ( performance   of  a  vocal   concert   program ) direction  53.03.03 "  Vocal   art » Levshina   Valeria   Program :  1. J. S.  Bach  –  Aria  " Quia   respexit "  from  vocal-symphonic  cycle  " Magnificat "  2. G.  fauré  , poems  R.  Bessin  – " Après   un   rêve " (" Awakening ")  3. A.  Dvorak  –  Mermaid's   Aria   from   the   Opera  " the   mermaid "  4. R. K.  Shchedrin  – " Natasha's   Song "  from   the   Opera  " Not   only   love "  5. P.I.  Tchaikovsky  , the   poetry   of   Goethe's  " Song   Minions "  6. P. I.  Tchaikovsky  –  Recitative   and   Iolanta's   Arioso   from   the   Opera  " Iolanta " Head   of   the  FQW:  Honored   People's   Artist   of   the  RSFSR,  Professor   Alexey   Steblyanko . Annotation . The   final   qualifying   work   is  a  vocal   concert   program   composed   of   classical   and   modern   works   of   Russian   and   foreign   composers .  The   program   includes   the   aria  " Quia   respexit "  from   Johann   Sebastian   Bach's   " Magnificat ",  which   represents   the   vocal   technique   of   Baroque   music .  The   foreign   opera   school   of   the   early  20th  century   is   represented   by   the   Aria   of   the   Mermaid   from   Antonin   Dvorak's   opera  " The   Mermaid ".  Gabriel   Faure's  " Après   un   rêve " (" The   Awakening ")  is  a  sample   of   foreign  ( French )  chamber   vocal   technique ,  while   Peter   Ilyich   Tchaikovsky's  " Song   of   the   Minions " ( translated   from   German   by   Fyodor   Ivanovich   Tyutchev )  reflects   Russian   chamber   vocal   technique .  The   program   also   includes   Recitative   and   arioso   of   Iolanta   from   the   opera  " Iolanta "  by   Peter   Ilyich   Tchaikovsky ,  as   an   example   of  a  prominent   Russian   opera   vocal   school .  The   program   concludes   with  " Natasha's   Song "  from   the   opera  " Not   Only   Love "  by   the   contemporary   composer   Rodion   Konstantinovich   Shchedrin .
